untrustedWorkspaces.description: Resolves op:// references from launch configurations. Only enable in workspaces you trust — launch configs can reference secrets from any vault the signed-in op CLI can read.

secretResolver.accountEmail.markdownDescription: Default 1Password account, looked up from this email address (case-insensitive). Overridden by the `SECRET_RESOLVER_ACCOUNT_EMAIL` launch env var. Lower priority than `#secretResolver.accountGitConfig#`, higher than `#secretResolver.accountId#`.

secretResolver.accountGitConfig.markdownDescription: Default 1Password account, resolved from the `user.email` in the git config of this workspace-relative subdirectory (`.` = workspace root; empty = off). Overridden by the `SECRET_RESOLVER_ACCOUNT_GIT_CONFIG` launch env var. Highest account-selection priority.

secretResolver.accountId.markdownDescription: Default 1Password account shorthand or UUID, used verbatim as `--account`. Overridden by the `SECRET_RESOLVER_ACCOUNT_ID` launch env var. Lowest account-selection priority.

secretResolver.opPath.description: Path to the 1Password CLI binary. Looked up on PATH when unqualified.

secretResolver.sanitizeVars.markdownDescription: Regular expression matching environment-variable **names** to strip from the launch environment before the program sees it. This is the only stripping applied; the default removes 1Password (`OP_*`) and `SECRET_RESOLVER_*` variables. Overridden by the `SECRET_RESOLVER_SANITIZE_VARS` launch env var; clear it (or set the env var empty) to disable stripping entirely.

secretResolver.signalOnStop.markdownDescription: Default stop-signal sequence for terminal launches: `+`-separated steps, each an optional `N:` second delay then a signal name (`TERM`, `KILL`, `INT`, `HUP`; case-insensitive), e.g. `TERM+5:KILL`. `off` or empty disables. Overridden by the `SECRET_RESOLVER_SIGNAL_ON_STOP` launch env var.

secretResolver.tokenTag.markdownDescription: Default tag of the "API Credential" vault item whose `credential` field is used as the service-account token for launches containing `op://` references. Overridden by the `SECRET_RESOLVER_TOKEN_TAG` launch env var.

description: Resolves 1Password secret references in VS Code launch configuration environment variables
name: vscode-secret-resolver
